2606.30473.title: Field Order Should Not Matter: Permutation-Invariant Embedding Model Fine-Tuning for Structured Metadata Retrieval
2606.30473.paper_summary: We study retrieval over catalogs of structured metadata, where each record is a small schema whose fields answer different kinds of query. Embedding a record with a text encoder first serializes its fields into a string, which forces a choice of field order. We show this choice, usually treated as an implementation detail, silently controls retrieval quality once the encoder is fine-tuned. A standard fine-tune loses 7.4 nDCG@10 points when the index is rebuilt under a different field order, because it reads absolute position instead of the field labels. We propose permutation-invariant fine-tuning ($\textbf{PI-FT}$), which serializes each record under a freshly sampled field order with random field dropout, so meaning binds to the labels rather than to position. The change is about two lines in the data loader; it costs negligible in-distribution accuracy and cuts the order-change penalty to 0.2 points. We study this in the discovery of development statistics, a catalog of nearly 10,000 indicators that should be searchable in many languages by a model small enough to self-host. As AI assistants and agents increasingly mediate access to public data and statistics, this retrieval step decides whether an answer is grounded in the right indicator or series, making discoverability a precondition for disseminating data through AI. Because usage logs cannot provide training signal for indicators no one has searched, we generate the queries instead. $\textbf{DevDataBench}$ is a fully LLM-generated benchmark of grounded, facet-targeted queries across 15 languages, covering every indicator for both training and evaluation. A fine-tuned 118M-parameter CPU encoder outperforms every zero-shot baseline, including $\texttt{text-embedding-3-large}$ (0.707 vs.\ 0.556 nDCG@10), with the largest gains in low-resource languages. We release the benchmark, pipeline, models, and a reusable PI-FT framework.
2606.30473.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究基於結構化元數據目錄的檢索，其中每條記錄都是一個小型架構，其欄位回答不同類型的查詢。首先，使用文本編碼器對記錄進行嵌入，將其欄位序列化為字符串，這迫使選擇欄位順序。我們顯示這一選擇，通常被視為實現細節，默默地控制著檢索質量，一旦編碼器被微調。當索引在不同的欄位順序下重建時，標準微調會損失7.4 nDCG@10點，因為它讀取的是絕對位置而不是欄位標籤。我們提出了不變排列微調（$\textbf{PI-FT}$），它在隨機欄位丟棄的情況下，根據新隨機抽樣的欄位順序序列化每條記錄，因此意義與標籤綁定，而不是與位置綁定。這一改變在數據加載器中僅需兩行代碼；它對分佈內準確度的影響微乎其微，並將順序變更的懲罰降低到0.2點。我們在開發統計的發現中研究這一點，這是一個包含近10,000個指標的目錄，應該能夠被一個足夠小以自我托管的模型以多種語言進行搜索。隨著AI助手和代理越來越多地調解對公共數據和統計的訪問，這一步檢索決定了一個答案是否基於正確的指標或系列，使得可發現性成為通過AI傳播數據的前提條件。由於使用日誌無法為沒有人搜索的指標提供訓練信號，我們反而生成查詢。$\textbf{DevDataBench}$是一個完全由LLM生成的基準，涵蓋15種語言的基於基礎的、面向特徵的查詢，涵蓋了所有指標以供訓練和評估。一個微調的118M參數CPU編碼器在每個零樣本基線中表現優於，包括$\texttt{text-embedding-3-large}$（0.707對0.556 nDCG@10），在低資源語言中獲得了最大的增益。我們發布了基準、管道、模型以及可重用的PI-FT框架。
2606.30473.author: Aivin V. Solatorio et.al.
2606.30473.authors: Aivin V. Solatorio, Olivier Dupriez, Rafael Macalaba
